channel.title: morningNewsSection1
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from morningNewsSection1, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Louisiana senator who voted to convict Trump loses Republican primary
item.description: Senator Bill Cassidy is one of the few remaining Republicans who voted to convict Trump after the Jan. 6 attack. Trump endorsed his opponent.

item.title: New York Magazine reviewing work of writer facing plagiarism allegations
item.description: The high-profile writer, author of a forthcoming book about Mayor Zohran Mamdani, is being investigated by the magazine after being accused of plagiarism.

item.title: The foreign fighters who helped topple Assad and why China worries about them
item.description: Thousands of Uyghurs became key fighters against Syria's Assad regime. For the first time, they agreed to be interviewed. NPR spent weeks with some of them.

item.title: New burn bans and Trump's battle with immigration and DEI are impacting forest fires
item.description: Firefighters say setting fires on purpose is one of the best ways to protect against massive wildfires later. But the Trump administration is banning this practice.
